mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.25 percent, restrictive monetary policy elevates corporate borrowing costs and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated FEDFUNDS combined with contracting industrial production (INDPRO) signals monetary overtightening that depresses corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of FEDFUNDS and INDPRO does not exhibit negative rank correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC > -0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a quarter-over-quarter slowdown in real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals fading demand momentum that precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 slowdown quarters in late-cycle regimes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer spending that directly reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS declines are not negative in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment (UNRATE) and falling payrolls (PAYEMS) confirm a labor market contraction that reduces aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS changes does not significantly predict negative 60-day returns (p-value > 0.05) in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0 percent restricts short-term liquidity and elevates money-market yields relative to equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals rising operating leverage and capacity utilization that boosts corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample observations.
